key_departures.impact: All-Pro caliber CB1 traded to Rams for 2026 first-round pick (No. 29), 2026 fifth-round pick, 2026 sixth-round pick, and a 2027 third-round pick. McDuffie immediately signed a 4-year, $124M extension with the Rams. Chiefs' best corner is gone.
Starting CB2 signed a 3-year, $51M deal with the Rams in free agency. Combined with the McDuffie trade, the Chiefs lost both starting cornerbacks from their 2022 draft class in the same week.

Starting safety signed a 3-year, $40.25M deal with the Bengals. Cook had 47 starts in 4 seasons and was the Chiefs' best safety in 2025. His departure leaves a major hole at S alongside unproven Chamarri Conner and Jaden Hicks.

Signed a 3-year, $24.75M deal with Washington. Chenal was a versatile LB starter (44 games in 4 seasons) and the eighth player from the Chiefs' 2022 draft class to depart. Leaves a depth/rotational hole at LB, though Nick Bolton and Drue Tranquill remain.

Signed a 2-year deal with the Titans. Was a depth/special teams CB who had fallen down the depth chart in recent seasons. The third CB from the 2022 draft class to leave, further thinning the cornerback room.

Signed a 1-year, $8.25M deal (with incentives) with Arizona. Backup QB who started one game after Mahomes' season-ending ACL tear in 2025. Departure creates a thin backup QB situation with Mahomes' recovery timeline uncertain.

Signed a 2-year deal with Detroit. Effectively rendered moot by the Kenneth Walker III signing. Was the lead RB in 2025 but no running back averaged more than 4 yards per carry. Departure is a net positive given Walker's arrival.

Released on March 4, saving $20M in cap space. Had one year remaining on a 4-year, $80M deal. Blocking had declined in 2025 and persistent penalty issues made the decision easier. Chiefs plan to start Jaylon Moore at RT. Creates a significant need at OT.
Released Feb. 23, clearing nearly $9M in cap space. Down year in 2025. Rotational EDGE loss deepens the pass rush need behind George Karlaftis and Chris Jones.
Chiefs declined to tender the restricted free agent at $3.52M. Fourth-string IOL; Hunter Nourzad and C.J. Hanson remain as interior depth. Minimal roster impact.

signings.analysis: Super Bowl LX MVP lands the largest guaranteed money ever for a running back in free agency. Three-year, $43.05M deal ($28.7M fully guaranteed) is elite-tier at RB. Walker forced 61 missed tackles in 2025 and had 10 rushes of 20+ yards — exactly the explosive threat the Chiefs desperately lacked. No KC RB averaged more than 4 yards per carry in 2025. This is a legitimate 3-year starter investment that fully addresses the RB need. Draft capital at RB is now unnecessary in early rounds.

Re-signing Kelce on a 1-year, $12M deal (max $15M with incentives) keeps the offense's best weapon and Mahomes' favorite target — Kelce led the team in targets (108), receptions (76), yards (851), and TDs (5) in 2025. However, a 1-year deal at age 36 is a bridge, not a solution. TE remains a future need given Kelce's age and the single-year commitment. The Chiefs will almost certainly want a developmental TE in this draft.

Three-year, $24.75M deal ($15M guaranteed) at mid-starter money brings a reliable veteran presence following the loss of Bryan Cook. Gilman, a former sixth-round pick, started 12 games for Baltimore after a midseason trade and helped stabilize their secondary. He'll be the lead safety alongside Chamarri Conner and Jaden Hicks. The deal is structured as a legitimate 2-year investment (only $15M guaranteed on a $24.75M deal signals team retains some outs). Partially addresses the S need — veterans Conner and Hicks are unproven, so the draft should still target S depth. Does not fully close the hole left by Cook's $40M deal.

Three-year, $21M deal ($14M guaranteed) adds run-stuffing depth alongside Chris Jones. Prior to this signing, the only notable IDL behind Jones was 2025 second-rounder Omarr Norman-Lott, who tore his ACL in October. Tonga had a career year with New England (24 tackles, 2 TFLs, 2 QB hits in 14 games) and also offers rare dual-use as a blocking fullback — a trait the Chiefs haven't had since Dontari Poe. At $7M AAV this is at the low end of starter-tier money for IDL; the deal addresses depth but the Chiefs will still want premium interior pass rush help via the draft or further free agency moves.

Re-signed on a 2-year, $11M deal (max $14M). Thornton was one of the Chiefs' best receivers in 2025 — a career-high 438 receiving yards on 19 receptions with 3 TDs in 14 games — particularly when Worthy and Rice were unavailable. At $5.5M AAV this is depth-tier money at WR. Retaining Thornton as a deep threat provides continuity with Mahomes but does not solve the WR room's overall lack of proven starters. WR remains a real draft need with Xavier Worthy coming off shoulder surgery and Rashee Rice's status uncertain.
Long snapper re-signed for his 12th season in Kansas City. Maintains special teams chemistry with Harrison Butker and Matt Araiza. Routine re-signing with no draft-need implications.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Kenneth Walker III (3yr/$43.05M, $28.7M gtd) is the largest guaranteed RB contract in free agency history and fully addresses this need. Walker is 25, the Super Bowl LX MVP, and the most explosive runner available. No early-round draft capital needed here.

The Chiefs lost ALL THREE starting-caliber CBs from their 2022 class in one week: Trent McDuffie (traded to Rams), Jaylen Watson (3yr/$51M to Rams), Joshua Williams (2yr deal to Titans). Zero notable CB signings made. Nohl Williams and Kristian Fulton remain but the depth chart is gutted. This is now the Chiefs' single most critical draft need. Top priority in rounds 1–2.

Bryan Cook departed (3yr/$40.25M to Bengals), leaving a large void. Alohi Gilman signed (3yr/$24.75M, $15M gtd) partially fills it — he's a legitimate starter and veteran leader. But Gilman's guarantee structure ($15M on $24.75M) signals modest investment, and the remaining depth (Conner, Hicks) is unproven. Safety remains a Tier 2 need — target mid-rounds in the draft.

Jawaan Taylor was released (saved $20M) and left tackle Josh Simmons was limited to 8 games as a rookie in 2025. Jaylon Moore is the plan at RT but has only 18 career starts. The offensive line protecting a recovering Mahomes (torn ACL/LCL) is a critical vulnerability. No FA OT signings made. Escalates to Tier 1 co-need alongside CB.

Mike Danna was cut, top free agent EDGE options (Boye Mafe, Odafe Oweh) signed elsewhere. George Karlaftis remains on his extension and Chris Jones can still generate interior pressure. No EDGE additions in FA. Still a meaningful need, unchanged at Tier 2 — should target EDGE in the draft.

Travis Kelce re-signed on a 1-year deal ($12M). This keeps the offense functional but it's a one-year bridge for a 36-year-old. The team has no proven TE depth behind Kelce. Need stays at Tier 3 — value pick in rounds 3–4 for a developmental option.

Khyiris Tonga (3yr/$21M, $14M gtd) meaningfully upgrades the rotation behind Chris Jones. Prior to this signing, the only depth was an ACL-recovery player (Norman-Lott). Tonga provides legitimate run-stopping presence and a dual-use fullback wrinkle. Drops one tier — still could use a pass-rushing IDL complement via draft Day 2.

Tyquan Thornton re-signed (2yr/$11M, depth money), but Xavier Worthy is recovering from shoulder surgery and Rashee Rice's status is uncertain. The core WR room — Worthy, Rice, Thornton, Royals — is talented but fragile and unproven at full strength. No significant WR addition was made. Remains Tier 2; likely a mid-round draft target.

Gardner Minshew departed (1yr/$8.25M to Cardinals). With Patrick Mahomes recovering from a torn ACL/LCL suffered in Week 15 of 2025, the Chiefs have no proven backup option. The backup QB market is essentially dry (Mariota, Huntley, Minshew all off the board). This is a real short-term risk until Mahomes' return timeline is clearer. Escalates to Tier 2 urgency — will need a veteran backup in FA Wave 2 or draft Day 3.

Leo Chenal signed with Washington (3yr/$24.75M), but Nick Bolton and Drue Tranquill remain on the roster as the top two LBs. Chenal was the third linebacker in a rotation — his departure hurts depth more than the starting lineup. LB stays at Tier 3, with a Day 3 pick for depth being logical.

key_additions.headline: Super Bowl LX MVP brings elite explosiveness to a run game that ranked 25th in the NFL in 2025
Veteran safety signed to stabilize a decimated secondary after the losses of Cook, McDuffie, and Watson
Two-way run-stopper and part-time fullback upgrades IDL depth alongside Chris Jones
Kelce returns for a 14th season; led the team in targets, receptions, yards, and TDs in 2025
Deep threat re-signed after career year (438 yards, 3 TDs) to provide WR stability behind Worthy and Rice

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded to LAR; received picks No. 29 (2026), R5, R6 (2026), R3 (2027)
key_losses.headline: All-Pro CB1 dealt for draft capital; now the highest-paid cornerback in NFL history at $31M/yr
Starting CB2 follows McDuffie to LA; both starting cornerbacks now play for the Rams
47-start safety departs for a homecoming in Cincinnati, leaving a major hole in the secondary
Versatile LB starter and eighth member of the 2022 draft class to leave Kansas City
Starting RT cut after blocking decline and persistent penalty issues; Jaylon Moore inherits the job
Backup QB departs, leaving the depth chart dangerously thin behind recovering Mahomes (torn ACL)
